book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

title: Chapter XXVII.

source.title: ANF06. Fathers of the Third Century: Gregory Thaumaturgus, Dionysius the Great, Julius Africanus, Anatolius, and Minor Writers, Methodius, Arnobius

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: We may give yet another illustration, if it seems good to you.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: A certain man, the head of a household, and possessed of great riches, was minded to journey abroad for a time, and promised to his sons that he would send them some one who would take his place, and divide among them equally the substance falling to them.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: And, in truth, not long after that, he did despatch to them a certain trustworthy and righteous and true man.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: And on his arrival, this man took charge of the whole substance, and first of all exerted himself to arrange it and administer it, giving himself great labour in journeying, and even working diligently with his own hands, and toiling like a servant for the good of the estate.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Afterwards feeling that his end was at hand, the man wrote out a will, demitting the inheritance to the relations and all the next of kin; and he gave them his seals, and called them together one by one by name, and charged them to preserve the inheritance, and to take care of the substance, and to administer it rightly, even as they had received it, and to take their use of its goods and fruits, as they were themselves left its owners and heirs.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: If, moreover, any person were to ask to be allowed to benefit by the fruits of this field, they were to show themselves indulgent to such.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: But if, on the other hand, any one were to declare himself partner in the heirship with them, and were to make his demands on that ground, they were to keep aloof from him, and pronounce him an alien; and further, they were to hold that the individual who desired to be received among them ought all the more on that account to do work.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Well, then, granting that all these things have been well and rightly disposed of and settled, and that they have continued in that condition for a very long time, how shall we deal with one who presents himself well-nigh three hundred years after, and sets up his claim to the heirship?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Shall we not cast him off from us?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Shall we not justly pronounce such a one an alien—one who cannot prove himself to have belonged to those related to our Master, who never was with our departed Lord in the hour of His sickness, who never walked in the funeral procession of the Crucified, who never stood by the sepulchre, who has no knowledge whatsoever of the manner or the character of His departure, and who, in fine, is now desirous of getting access to the storehouse of corn without presenting any token from him who placed it under lock and seal?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Shall we not cast him off from us like a robber and a thief, and thrust him out of our number by all possible means?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Yet this man is now in our presence, and fails to produce any of the credentials which we have summarized in what we have already said, and declares that he is the Paraclete whose mission was presignified by Jesus.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: And by this assertion, in his ignorance perchance, he will make out Jesus Himself to be a liar; for thus He who once said that He would send the Paraclete no long time after, will be proved only to have sent this person, if we accept the testimony which he bears to himself, after an interval of three hundred years and more.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: In the day of judgment, then, what will those say to Jesus who have departed this life from that time on to the present period?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Will they not meet Him with words like these: “Do not punish us rigorously if we have failed to do Thy works.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: For why, when Thou didst promise to send the Paraclete under Tiberius Cæsar, to convince us of sin and of righteousness, didst Thou send Him only under Probus the Roman emperor, and didst leave us orphaned, not withstanding that Thou didst say, ‘I will not leave you comfortless (orphaned),’ and after Thou hadst also assured us that Thou wouldest send the Paraclete presently after Thy departure?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: What could we orphans do, having no guardian?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: We have committed no fault; it is Thou that hast deceived us.”

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: But away with such a supposition in the case of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Saviour of every soul.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: For He did not confine Himself to mere promises; but when He had once said, “I go to my Father, and I send the Paraclete to you,” straightway He sent (that gift of the Paraclete), dividing and imparting the same to His disciples,—bestowing it, however, in greater fulness upon Paul.
